steps.title: Identify what both texts directly describe about Asimov's characters
steps.content: Both authors explicitly observe the same characteristic: Asimov's characters focus on ideas and intellectual discussion rather than emotions and personal lives. Text 1 states characters “discuss ideas rather than emotions.” Text 2 describes characters “discussing science and politics” with little revealed about “characters' lives.” This shared observation is the deciding signal.

steps.title: Match the common observation to the choices
steps.content: Choice C captures exactly what both texts state—that Asimov's characters are concerned mainly with intellectual matters rather than emotions. Choices A and D involve unsupported comparisons or assessments; choice B mischaracterizes the alignment because Text 1 explicitly accepts the style as “mediocre” while Text 2 calls the criticism “wrong,” showing they disagree on fairness. That matches choice C — C is correct.
